data.title: Payment software company adds stablecoin settlement to its stack, combining USDG, USDP, USDC with ACH and wires. #Fintech #Innovation
data.description: The payment software firm has incorporated stablecoin settlement into its current payment system, merging USDG, USDP, and USDC with conventional payment methods. This update aims to enhance the efficiency and flexibility of transactions within the company, offering users more choices for settling payments.

data.title: JCDecaux introduces holistic tool globally to measure full campaign impact, enhancing visibility.
data.description: JCDecaux has introduced a comprehensive tool to evaluate the complete impact of campaigns globally. This tool will measure aspects that are not immediately visible, offering a more thorough analysis of campaign effectiveness. This initiative will be rolled out worldwide, emphasizing the importance of assessing all campaign elements for an accurate evaluation.

data.title: Palo Alto Networks stock slides as Q2 results disappoint analysts. Various price targets reduced, with organic NGS ARR up 28% YoY.
data.description: Shares of Palo Alto Networks Inc (NASDAQ: PANW) plummeted after the company's fiscal second-quarter results. Various analysts adjusted their ratings and price targets for the company. Needham analyst Mike Cikos maintained a Buy rating but decreased the price target from $230 to $200. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs analyst Gabriela Borges reiterated a Buy rating and cut the price target from $240 to $224. Scotiabank analyst Patrick Colville reaffirmed a Sector Outperform rating, adjusting the price target from $228 to $180. DA Davidson analyst Rudy Kessinger reiterated a Buy rating, with the price target lowered from $210 to $190. Rosenblatt Securities' Catharine Trebnick and BTIG's Gray Powell maintained Buy ratings with price targets of $225 and $200, respectively. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives kept an Outperform rating and price target of $225, while Citizens JMP Securities analyst Trevor Walsh reiterated a Market Outperform rating and a $250 price target. Needham highlighted Palo Alto Networks' solid results, with organic NGS ARR growing 28% YoY to $6.14 billion (excluding $200 million from Chronosphere). The company's guidance for NGS ARR in the fiscal third quarter was at $7.94-$7.96 billion, including $1.47 billion from acquisitions, aligning with consensus on an organic basis. On the other hand, Goldman Sachs noted that Palo Alto Networks reported NGS ARR 3% above estimates, largely due to the Chronosphere acquisition. The stock faced pressure due to limited organic growth and "inorganic moving pieces."

data.title: Cardio Diagnostics Holdings to hold investor call on 2/19/2026 at 3pm CT.
data.description: Cardio Diagnostics is set to hold an investor conference call on February 19, 2026, at 3 p.m. CT in Chicago.

data.title: CCA named Jean Ann and Barry C. McCarthy recipients of 2026 Bishop’s Award for 2 decades of charity work; gala on Feb 21 at Georgia Aquarium.
data.description: In Atlanta, Catholic Charities announced Jean Ann and Barry C. McCarthy as recipients of the 2026 Bishop’s Award for their 20-year dedication to aiding the less fortunate. The award ceremony will take place on February 21, 2026, at the Georgia Aquarium's Oceans Ballroom during the Annual Gala Soiree. This award is the highest honor given by Catholic Charities Atlanta and acknowledges outstanding contributions to the community.

data.description: Soho Square Capital has confirmed that Netomnia, a portfolio company, is set to be acquired by InfraVia, Liberty Global, and Telefónica. This acquisition agreement marks a significant development within the industry.

data.title: Alcoa stock rises as it modernizes mining approvals in Australia to enhance sustainability and environmental practices.
data.description: Alcoa's shares have risen on Wednesday as the company announced plans to modernize its mining approvals framework in Australia. The agreement with the Australian Federal Government involves a Strategic Assessment for mining areas until 2045 to assess environmental impacts and improve operational clarity. Alcoa will operate at Huntly and Willowdale mines during the assessment with a National Interest Exemption. The company aims to enhance sustainability practices and maintain its position in the aluminum market through this initiative. Alcoa plans to limit clearing to 800 hectares annually and increase rehabilitation efforts to 1,000 hectares by 2027, showing its commitment to environmental sustainability.

data.title: HF Sinclair stock drops 14% as CEO Tim Go takes leave. Franklin Myers appointed interim CEO. Q4 earnings: $221M net income. Audit review ongoing.
data.description: HF Sinclair Corp. (NYSE: DINO) experienced a significant drop in stock price after CEO Tim Go took a voluntary leave of absence, prompting the audit committee to review disclosure controls. Board Chair Franklin Myers has been appointed interim CEO. The stock plummeted by nearly 14% following the news. Despite this, the company's unaudited 2025 earnings were released, and the annual report is expected to be filed on schedule. The audit committee's review is stated not to impact the recently announced financial results, which revealed a positive fourth-quarter earnings rebound with adjusted net income of $221 million, $1.20 per diluted share, compared to a loss in the same period last year. Executives did not provide further details on the leadership change or review during the earnings call. Myers mentioned updates will be shared in due course.

data.title: Protectt.ai launches new version of AppProtectt in Dubai to enhance mobile app security platform. #AIRedTeaming.
data.description: In Dubai, Protectt.ai unveiled the newest iteration of AppProtectt during an event.

data.title: Winmark Corp (Nasdaq: WINA) reports year end net income of $41,654,100, up from $39,954,200 in 2024, with revenues of $86,055,700.
data.description: Winmark Corporation (Nasdaq: WINA) revealed its financial results for the year ending December 27, 2025. The company reported a net income of $41,654,100, equivalent to $11.30 per share diluted, an increase from the previous year's $39,954,200 or $10.89 per share diluted in 2024. In the fourth quarter of 2025, net income was $9,959,900, translating to $2.69 per share diluted, compared to $9,583,100 or $2.60 per share diluted in the same period the prior year. Revenues for the year ending December 27, 2025, amounted to $86,055,700, up from $81,289.
